frameworks.name: ideal observer analysis
frameworks.one_line: Derives expected behavior of an optimal but capacity-limited memory system

frameworks.name: rate-distortion theory
frameworks.one_line: Information-theoretic bound on accuracy at fixed information capacity

frameworks.name: fixed item-limit models
frameworks.one_line: Slot-based VWM models with a hard cap on stored items

frameworks.name: variable-number storage model
frameworks.one_line: Allows variability in number of stored representations without fixed item limit

core_question: What are the principled limits on visual working memory under a fixed information capacity?
key_assumptions: Visual working memory has a fixed information-transmission capacity
An optimal memory system trades distortion against capacity
Behavior approximates the optimum subject to capacity constraints
manipulations: Empirical tasks designed to probe ideal-observer predictions
Comparison of alternative VWM models within the same framework
Variation of memory load implicit in task structure
key_findings: Ideal observer analysis yields novel quantitative predictions for VWM
Two empirical studies confirm the predicted performance bounds
A model allowing variable number of stored representations fits the data well
No fixed item limit is required to account for empirical VWM data
interpretations: VWM capacity is best characterized as bounded information rate rather than discrete item slots
Existing slot-limit models can be reinterpreted as special cases of a rate-distortion account
